description_long: This object is a classic British police telephone box, most famously known as the TARDIS from Doctor Who. It features a tall, rectangular structure with a flat roof and a small, cylindrical light fixture on top. The exterior is painted a deep navy blue, with paneled doors and white-framed windows on the upper half of each side. The front displays the iconic 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' signage in white letters on a black background above the windows, and a small instruction panel is visible on one of the doors. The booth is constructed primarily from wood or metal, with glass window panes and a likely hollow interior. Its robust build and enclosed design make it suitable for outdoor urban environments.
description: A tall, blue British police telephone box with white-paned windows, a black-and-white sign, and a small light on top. The front features signage and an instruction panel, with a sturdy, enclosed structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' sign and two white-paned windows, with no visible instruction panel.
description_view_1: Front view with the 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' sign, two white-paned windows, and a visible instruction panel on the left door.
description_view_2: Side view similar to the front, with the 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' sign and windows, but no instruction panel visible.
description_view_3: Rear or alternate side view, showing the 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' sign and windows, with no instruction panel.
attribute_window_configuration: two rectangular white-paned windows on each visible side, upper half of the structure
attribute_signage_or_labeling: White 'POLICE PUBLIC CALL BOX' sign on a black background above the windows; instruction panel on the left door
